title: Multiply by 5 and 10
description: Multiply by 5 or 10 times a number less than or equal to 10.

hints:
We can think of $\blueD{5} \times \greenD{1}$ as $\blueD5$ rows of $\greenD1$ circle. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{5} \times \greenD{1} = \purpleC{5}$

We can think of $\blueD{5} \times \greenD{5}$ as $\blueD5$ rows of $\greenD5$ circles. How many circles are there?

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD5\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD5}\\
\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{25}
\end{align}$

We can think of $\blueD{5} \times \greenD{8}$ as $\blueD5$ rows of $\greenD8$ circles. How many circles are there?

[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{5} \times \greenD{8} = \purpleC{40}$

We can think of $\blueD{5} \times \greenD{9}$ as $\blueD5$ rows of $\greenD9$ circles. How many circles are there?



[[☃ image 1]]
$\Large\blueD{5} \times \greenD{9} = \purpleC{45}$

We can think of $\blueD{5} \times \greenD{2}$ as $\blueD5$ rows of $\greenD2$ circles. How many circles are there?


[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}&\phantom{\Large{\times}}\Large\blueD5\\
\\
&\underline{\Large\times\Large\greenD2}\\
\\
&~~\Large\purpleC{10}
\end{align}$
